units.description: Input precondition: the slice is non-empty. (Variant 6 stresses adversarial small inputs (n<=3 style) while preserving asymptotic-agnostic correctness.)
common_failure_modes: treating the empty-slice case as returning 0

units.description: Existential witness: there is a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned value. (Variant 6 stresses adversarial small inputs (n<=3 style) while preserving asymptotic-agnostic correctness.)
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound without a witness
quantifying over subsequences rather than contiguous subslices

units.description: Upper-bound property: no contiguous non-empty subslice sums strictly above the returned value. (Variant 6 stresses adversarial small inputs (n<=3 style) while preserving asymptotic-agnostic correctness.)
common_failure_modes: quantifying only over subslices of a fixed length

units.description: Contiguity constraint: the witness is a contiguous range i..j, not a subsequence. (Variant 6 stresses adversarial small inputs (n<=3 style) while preserving asymptotic-agnostic correctness.)
common_failure_modes: allowing gaps in the witnessed indices

units.description: All-negative case: the result equals the maximum single element when every element is negative. (Variant 6 stresses adversarial small inputs (n<=3 style) while preserving asymptotic-agnostic correctness.)
